channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Instagram’s AI bots are often sexually suggestive—and sometimes underage
item.description: When Meta launched its “AI Studio” feature for over two billion Instagram users in July 2024, the company promised a tool that would give anyone the ability to create their own AI characters “to make you laugh, generate memes, give travel advice, and so much more.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Archaeologists unearth the remains of a Roman basilica on the site of a new London skyscraper
item.description: Work to give London yet another skyscraper has uncovered traces of the city’s origins almost 2,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SAP aims to unify data for AI, analytics with new Business Data Cloud
item.description: With its new Business Data Cloud, SAP wants to help enterprises unify data held in its own systems with that in other vendors’ applications: It’s goal is to expedite advanced analytics and AI use cases.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple event rumors – here’s when we could see the iPhone SE 4, MacBook Air M4 and more
item.description: The Apple rumors are flying ahead of possible launches for the iPhone SE 4 and M4 MacBook Air –here's when they could land and how likely we think an Apple Spring event is.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Paramount is leaving YouTube TV – but YouTube is cutting some bills as compensation
item.description: YouTube TV subscribers are about to lose Paramount channels – and there’s no sign of when they’ll return.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Innovation in AI is in danger of outpacing governance
item.description: Fast AI innovation comes at the expense of governance, with many leaders worried about a lack of AI frameworks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 14 questioni che tolgono il sonno ai CIO
item.description: I CIO, al giorno d’oggi, sono molto stressati dalle preoccupazioni di vecchia data sulla rapidità del cambiamento e sulla rivoluzione quantistica all’orizzonte. Naturalmente, ci sono anche molti altri problemi che preoccupano i CIO qui e ora.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meet the Bop House, the internet’s divisive new OnlyFans hype house
item.description: What if the Playboy Mansion was filled with OnlyFans content creators? That’s the pitch for the Bop House, a TikTok page that has gained nearly three million followers since its launch in December.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s SBM publishes blueprint for AI success
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 emphasizes a technology-driven transformation to achieve its social, economic, and environmental goals, focusing on adopting and producing advanced innovations to position itself as a global leader in technological disruption.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CISPE raises red alert over Broadcom’s VMware licensing changes
item.description: The European Cloud Competition Observatory (ECCO), a monitoring body established by the association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) as a way to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region, this week came down hard on Broadcom for its failure to do so.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump’s firing of the U.S. government archivist is far worse than it might seem
item.description: Amid his dramatic reshaping of the federal government, President Donald Trump’s latest high-profile dismissal could become among his most consequential. On Feb.
item.dc:creator: Jared Keller, Fast Company

item.title: SEC and Binance request 60-day pause in lawsuit as the agency shifts to be more crypto-friendly
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is seeking to pause its high-profile lawsuit against the cryptocurrency exchange Binance as the regulator tries to present itself as more crypto-friendly under a new administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Gulf of Mexico Renamed to Gulf of America on Apple Maps
item.description: President Trump's order to rename the Gulf of Mexico is now reflected in Apple maps, following Google and Microsoft's lead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: House Commerce chair launches data privacy working group
item.description: House Committee on Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) on Wednesday announced the creation of a data privacy working group as policymakers work toward a national privacy standard.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Showing memory usage in Linux by process and user
item.description: There are a lot of tools for looking at memory usage on Linux systems. Some are commonly used commands like ps, while others are tools like top that allow you to display system performance stats in various ways.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Looking for love this Valentine's Day? Don't fall for Instagram romance scams
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms is urging users to stay vigilant about “romance scams” ahead of Valentine’s Day, warning of unsolicited messages through its apps and other social media platforms, as well as general text messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 3 reasons Microsoft needn’t fear DeepSeek
item.description: The release of the latest version of the Chinese genAI bot DeepSeek last month upended the tech world when its creators claimed it was built for only $6 million — far less than the hundreds of billions of dollars Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Meta, and others have poured into genAI development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Monday.com aims to be an ‘AI-First’ platform with latest enhancements
item.description: Monday.com has accelerated its push into artificial intelligence (AI) with the announcement this week of its AI vision, which includes three areas of focus: AI Blocks, embedded Product Power-ups, and a forthcoming Digital Workforce of AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner
item.description: The National Association of State CIOs and Deloitte outlined five calls to action in a recent survey of state chief information security officers. The post On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DeepSeek banned from NY state government devices
item.description: New York State is prohibiting the China-based artificial intelligence application DeepSeek from government devices. New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Google’s generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help connect agents with databases
item.description: Google’s cloud computing division, Google Cloud, has added a new generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help enterprise developers connect agent-based generative AI applications to its databases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
